name: tutorial-builder
description: Turns code, a feature, or a library into a step-by-step, hands-on tutorial that teaches rather than references. Runs a pedagogical workflow: define measurable learning objectives (Bloom's verbs, not 'understand'), decompose the topic into atomic concepts ordered simple to complex with no forward references, and design exercises that scaffold I-do / We-do / You-do. Produces a locked tutorial structure - opening (what you'll learn, prerequisites, time estimate, final result, setup), progressive sections (concept, minimal example, guided practice, variations, challenges, troubleshooting), and a closing (summary, next steps, resources, call to action). Applies retention patterns (learn by doing, spaced repetition, worked examples, immediate feedback, analogies), cognitive-load limits (7 plus-or-minus 2, one-screen code, one new concept per step), and five exercise types calibrated by difficulty (fill-in-the-blank, debug, extension, from-scratch, refactoring). Every code block must run, show expected output, and be explained; a pre-publish quality checklist and a 1-5 speed score gate the result. A bundled implementation playbook carries a full worked tutorial, per-format deep-dives, an exercise bank, and an expanded review rubric; a sample tutorial ships as a copy-ready target. Scope-locked to teaching content. Not for API reference documentation or marketing copy. Includes a /tutorial-builder slash command with a 5-field guided intake.
